data.title: Chinese Associations Warn Against Illegal Activities Involving Virtual Currencies
data.description:
According to Foresight News, the China Internet Finance Association, along with six other associations, issued a joint risk warning today regarding illegal activities related to virtual currencies. Previously, on April 13, 2022, the China Internet Finance Association had released an initiative to prevent financial risks associated with NFTs, emphasizing the need to curb the financialization and securitization of NFTs and to strictly guard against illegal financial activities.

The associations urged their member units not to invest directly or indirectly in NFTs and to refrain from providing financing support for NFT investments. They also advised against using methods such as fractional ownership or mass creation to undermine the non-fungible characteristics of NFTs, which could lead to disguised token issuance financing (ICO). Furthermore, they cautioned against including financial assets such as securities, insurance, credit, or precious metals in the underlying goods of NFTs, which could result in the issuance and trading of financial products in disguise.

data.title: XRP News: XRP Slides Below Key Support as Analysts Warn of Potential Retest of $2.05 Despite Strong ETF Inflows
data.description: Ripple’s XRP entered a deeper corrective phase on Tuesday after breaking below the critical $2.07 support zone, a level that had held multiple intraday retests earlier in the week. The move comes despite accelerating institutional participation, with spot XRP ETFs surpassing $850 million in inflows since launching in mid-November — one of the strongest altcoin ETF debuts on record.The divergence between strengthening fundamental demand and weakening technical structure has sharpened trader focus on the next major support at $2.05, a level several analysts warn may now be at risk.Institutional Demand Builds, but Market Liquidity Remains ThinXRP continues to face a split environment:ETF flows remain robust, with long-horizon capital steadily accumulating exposure.Speculative activity, however, has cooled, with declining open interest across major venues signaling a more risk-off posture.Bitcoin’s inability to reclaim key weekly levels has added pressure across the altcoin complex.This mismatch — strong institutional inflows versus restrained retail and derivatives participation — has left XRP more vulnerable to technical breakdowns, despite improving fundamentals.Technical Breakdown: $2.07 Support Fails on Expanding VolumeXRP attempted to stabilize above the $2.07–$2.11 band throughout the session, but repeated lower highs signaled waning buyer momentum. Every rejection near $2.11–$2.13 came with rising volume — a classic indication of seller control.The decisive shift occurred in the session’s final hour:$2.07 collapsed on a high-volume spike, confirming a structural breakdown.A secondary burst at 03:24 GMT pushed XRP toward the $2.00 handle, validating that the move was not a false break.Former support at $2.07 has now flipped into immediate resistance.Momentum indicators have turned firmly bearish, with RSI drifting lower and MACD accelerating into negative territory.Price Action SnapshotXRP dropped from $2.20 → $2.10, a 5.7% decline across a $0.13, 24-hour range.A volume spike at 19:00 UTC — 68% above normal — marked the rejection at $2.13.Price briefly traded near $2.00 before recovering to the $2.10–$2.12 band.XRP remains below all intraday resistance levels, keeping downside momentum intact.Key Levels to WatchImmediate resistance:$2.07–$2.11 — must be reclaimed with conviction to reestablish bullish structure.Downside targets:$2.05 — analysts flag as the next critical retest.$1.90–$1.97 — deeper demand region if $2.05 fails.November lows — would come into play if bearish pressure accelerates.Despite ETF inflows providing longer-term support, institutional accumulation did not offset the short-term structural weakening observed on intraday charts.Market OutlookXRP now sits at a pivotal technical juncture. A clean bounce from $2.05, paired with a decisive reclaim of $2.11, would be the earliest constructive signal that buyers are regaining strength.Until then, the near-term bias remains cautious:Trend: BearishMomentum: BearishStructure: Below support, resistance overheadETF flows: Strong but not yet driving price stabilization

data.title: EU Proposes Shift in Cryptocurrency Regulation to Enhance Market Oversight
data.description: According to Odaily, the European Commission has proposed transferring regulatory authority over cryptocurrency companies to the European Union's market regulatory body as part of a comprehensive financial market integration initiative. The Commission believes that incorporating cryptocurrency regulation into the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) will enhance regulatory efficiency and facilitate cross-border activities. This move aims to address the fragmentation caused by differing regulatory approaches among the 27 member states. Previously, individual national regulators, including France's AMF, Austria's FMA, and Italy's Consob, expressed concerns about the implementation of the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) framework and called for ESMA to strengthen its oversight. The proposal is subject to negotiation and approval by the European Parliament and the European Council.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Outlook Faces Uncertainty Amid Federal Reserve Caution
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Bank of America strategists have issued a warning that excessive caution from the Federal Reserve regarding economic prospects could jeopardize the anticipated year-end rally in the stock market. As the S&P 500 index approaches its historical peak, investors are hopeful for a rate cut from the Federal Reserve alongside a decline in inflation. However, Bank of America strategist Michael Hartnett has highlighted that if the Federal Reserve signals a dovish stance in its upcoming meeting, this optimism may be challenged, potentially indicating a greater-than-expected economic slowdown.

data.title: US CFTC Approves Spot Crypto Trading on Regulated Exchanges
data.description:
The CFTC has approved spot cryptocurrency trading on U. S. regulated futures exchanges, aiming to protect investors and strengthen America’s role in global crypto markets. The post US CFTC Approves Spot Crypto Trading on Regulated Exchanges first appeared on The Shib Daily.
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/12/05/us-cftc-approves-spot-crypto-trading-on-regulated-exchanges/

data.title: Florida Court Lets $80M Binance Bitcoin Lawsuit Move Forward
data.description:
A Florida appeals court revived a lawsuit claiming Binance failed to act after an $80 million Bitcoin theft, allowing the plaintiff to pursue state-level claims and signaling increased legal scrutiny on crypto platforms. The post Florida Court Lets $80M Binance Bitcoin Lawsuit Move Forward first appeared on The Shib Daily.
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/12/05/florida-court-lets-80m-binance-bitcoin-lawsuit-move-forward/

data.title: Social Media Platform X Fined by EU for Content Regulation Breach
data.description: According to Foresight News, social media platform X has been fined 120 million euros by the European Union for violating content regulations. The European Commission stated that X breached three provisions of the Digital Services Act. The company has been given 60 days to propose a solution and 90 days to implement it.

data.title: USPD Contract Suffers $1 Million Loss in Prolonged Attack
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, CertiK Alert has reported a security breach involving the USPD contract, resulting in a loss of approximately $1 million. The attack, which spanned two months, involved manipulation of stored data. Analysts have identified two phases in the attack: initially, the attacker executed a legitimate initialization transaction to insert a malicious intermediary proxy into the USPD stabilizer. Subsequently, the attacker used this proxy to grant privileged roles to their contract, exploiting these privileges 78 days later to carry out the attack.

data.title: Bitcoin Price Could Hit $170K — But Strategy ‘Resilience’ Is Vital: JPMorgan
data.description:
JPMorgan’s newest forecast places Bitcoin on course for $170,000, but cautions that renewed gains depend on market “resilience”, including a calm futures market and stable volatility dynamics.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/strategys-ability-to-hold-bitcoin-may-shape-btcs-path-to-170k-jpmorgan/

data.title: China and India drive equity surge as investors leave the U.S.

But growing worries about sky-high technology prices could slow things down. Deals involving Asian stocks, including new company listings, follow-up sales, and convertible bonds, reached $267 billion through this year. That […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/china-and-india-drive-equity-surge/

data.title: OTC Weekly Trading Insights (12/05/2025): Fed stop QT, BOJ prepare for rate hike
data.description: Overall MarketData source: TradingViewIn our previous report, we identified the key drivers for Bitcoin (BTC) and the broader crypto market in the coming months: the Federal Reserve’s anticipated rate cut and dovish pivot in December, alongside the Bank of Japan’s (BOJ) potential rate hike on December 19. These two central banks played a central role in last week’s market movements. We also highlighted the $93,000 resistance level as a significant barrier, limiting BTC’s upward momentum amid persistent selling pressure above this threshold.On the central bank front, the Federal Reserve’s dovish shift, fueled by speculation around President Trump’s potential Fed Chair nominee in early 2026, signals a move toward looser monetary policy. Kevin Hassett, a former White House economic advisor and Trump loyalist, is emerging as a frontrunner. Markets expect Hassett to adopt a dovish stance, possibly delivering two rate cuts to bring the federal funds rate down to 3-3.25% by year-end, though this remains speculative. Softening labor market data, such as the recent ADP report showing a loss of 32,000 jobs in November, along with slowing consumer demand, further support the Fed’s inclination toward easing.In contrast, the Bank of Japan is moving closer to a rate hike, driven by persistent inflation, including Tokyo’s core CPI rising 2.8% year-over-year in November. This move could attract capital back to Japan, tightening global liquidity. Higher rates would also increase carry trade costs, potentially triggering another round of unwind. Our desk views the market impact as likely more muted than January 2025’s hike to 0.50%, given that expectations have been building for months. Nevertheless, the ongoing liquidity squeeze remains a headwind for risk assets, especially liquidity-sensitive ones like BTC and crypto assets.From a macroeconomic perspective, we expect heightened volatility during the holiday season, amplified by the Fed’s rate decision on December 10 and the BOJ’s on December 19. Analysts currently price in an 80-90% probability of a 25-basis-point Fed rate cut, likely accompanied by a hawkish statement from Chair Powell to temper expectations. For the BOJ, there is a 76-80% chance of a 25-basis-point hike, reflected in the Japan 10-year government bond yield rising to 1.94%, its highest level since the 2008 Global Financial Crisis.Our analysis confirms that the $93,000 resistance level for BTC remains firm, constraining further gains as selling pressure intensifies above it. A decisive break above $93,000, followed by a successful retest as support, would shift our outlook to bullish, potentially opening the path toward $98,000–$100,000.That said, with thinning liquidity over the holidays and major central bank decisions approaching, we anticipate volatile price action amid reduced trading volumes. This environment could lead to rapid momentum shifts. Looking ahead, the positive global liquidity outlook for 2026 underpins our bullish stance, with expectations for a meaningful BTC and crypto rally in the first quarter.Bitcoin ETF TrackerThe above table shows the daily BTC spot ETF net inflow data for the past five trading sessions.Last week, the US market experienced a strong capital inflow through ETFs following the largest outflow week. The Bitcoin ETF sector saw $123 million in inflows over the last four trading days, as the market being closed last Thursday for Thanksgiving.BTC price reached the $93,000 level on Friday, driven by strong ETF demand, but the gains were erased on Monday, December 1, amid rising expectations of a Bank of Japan rate hike. Despite the bearish sentiment, BTC ETFs still recorded an $8.48 million inflow on Monday, indicating that investors view this price as a buying opportunity. BTC surged on Tuesday, supported by a dovish outlook on the Federal Reserve’s stance in 2026, alongside a $58.50 million capital inflow into BTC ETFs.Based on last week’s data, our desk believes US ETF investors are closely following BTC price movements and are actively buying on dips.Macro at a glanceWeekly Macro Highlights (November 27-December 3, 2025)Friday, November 28, 2025Tokyo Core CPI rose 2.8% year-over-year in November, steady from October and slightly above the 2.7% consensus. This persistent inflation above the Bank of Japan’s 2% target strengthens expectations for a potential rate hike soon, signaling a hawkish shift that could tighten global liquidity. Higher Japanese rates may also boost the yen and pressure carry trades, creating headwinds for risk assets.Monday, December 2, 2025The S&P Global US Manufacturing PMI for November came in at 52.2, beating the expected 51.9 and marking the fourth straight month of expansion. In contrast, the ISM US Manufacturing PMI fell to 48.2, below the forecasted 49.0, indicating a slight acceleration in contraction.This divergence highlights mixed signals in US manufacturing: pockets of resilience per S&P Global, but broader weakness driven by soft new orders and employment per ISM. High interest rates and slowing global demand continue to challenge the sector, potentially weighing on overall economic growth.Tuesday, December 3, 2025Eurozone headline CPI inflation accelerated to 2.2% year-over-year in November, up from 2.1% in October and slightly above expectations. Core CPI remained steady at 2.4%.The unemployment rate held at 6.4% in October, unchanged from September but above forecasts and last year’s level.Rising services prices keep inflation near the ECB’s 2% target, signaling persistent pressures that may limit aggressive rate cuts. Meanwhile, the stable yet elevated unemployment rate points to a softening labor market, raising concerns about economic stagnation and supporting the case for further ECB easing to stimulate demand.Wednesday, December 4, 2025US ADP Nonfarm Employment unexpectedly declined by 32,000 private-sector jobs in November, contrasting with forecasts for a 5,000 gain. October’s figure was revised up to +47,000.This is the third job loss in four months and the largest drop since early pandemic disruptions, reflecting labor market weakness amid high borrowing costs and economic uncertainty. The weak report fueled market expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts in December, boosting stocks and risk assets as investors anticipate looser policy to counter slowing employment.Why trade OTC?  Binance offers our clients various ways to access OTC trading, including chat communication channels and the Binance OTC platform (https://www.binance.com/en/otc) for manual price quotations and execution services. Email: trading@binance.com for more information.Join our Telegram Channel @BinanceOTCTrading (https://t.me/+0mkJQnbQiOdlZjk0) to stay up to date with the markets!You can also reach out to us on Telegram (@Binance_OTC_Desk) to connect directly with our experienced traders for your OTC trading needs. We look forward to assisting you and providing exceptional service for all your OTC transactions.

data.title: Aave Community Approves Strategy Adjustment for V3 Deployment
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the Aave community has overwhelmingly approved a temperature check vote to adjust the V3 multi-chain deployment strategy, with a support rate of 99.96%. The proposal suggests closing Aave V3 deployments on zkSync, Metis, and Soneium, while setting a $2 million revenue threshold for new deployment chains. The community plans to release a standard ARFC proposal following this decision.

data.title: Fidelity CEO Abigail Johnson Expresses Support for Bitcoin
data.description: According to PANews, Fidelity CEO Abigail Johnson has expressed her support for Bitcoin, stating, "I like Bitcoin. I own Bitcoin. It will play an important role in the savings system."

data.title: Cloudflare Completes Maintenance at Detroit Data Center
data.description: According to PANews, Cloudflare has announced the completion of maintenance at its Detroit data center. The company stated that it is currently monitoring the results following the scheduled maintenance, which took place on December 5 from 17:00 to 21:00 UTC+8.
